title: tulipa hungarica subsp. undulatifolia
intro: Tulipa hungarica subsp. undulatifolia, also known as the wavy-leafed Hungarian tulip, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the lily family native to the Pannonian region of Central Europe. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Tulipa hungarica subsp. undulatifolia include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as exhibiting potential antimicrobial activity. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
